name: Angus Tenderloin with Sauteed Mushrooms and Texas Caviar
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the outside of the tenderloin with seasoned salt and pepper.
2. Preheat oven to 225 degrees Fahrenheit and roast for 45 minutes.
3. Slice the beef into 1/2-inch slices.
4. Serve each portion with 3 ounces sliced beef, half a cup of sauteed mushrooms, and half a cup of Texas Caviar.
SAUTEED MUSHROOMS:
1. Melt butter in a skillet until it becomes hot.
2. Add the mushrooms and onions to the skillet and cook them until the onions are tender.
3. Stir in garlic salt, chicken broth, and beef broth.
4. Allow the mixture to simmer for 15 minutes.
TEXAS CAVIAR:
1. In a bowl, combine black-eyed peas, chopped tomato, green onions, minced garlic, finely chopped green bell pepper, chopped cilantro, mild picante sauce, salt, and pepper.
2. Mix well, cover, and chill in the refrigerator for 24 hours before serving.
